name: Ketamine

dosing: Intranasal doses typically range from 15-50 mg per spray, used as an acute abortive at attack onset.
IV infusions are administered in clinical settings at provider-determined doses, often as a series of treatments.
Oral troches or lozenges are prescribed at 75-100 mg for at-home maintenance use.
Esketamine (Spravato) nasal spray is FDA-approved for depression and used off-label by some CH patients.
preparations: Intranasal spray is the most common form, either compounded by a pharmacy or as prescription Spravato.
IV infusions are administered in specialized ketamine clinics under medical supervision.
Oral troches or lozenges dissolve in the mouth and are used for at-home dosing.
schedule: For acute use, intranasal spray is taken at attack onset and repeated every 15 minutes for up to 3-4 doses.
IV infusion protocols typically involve 6-7 sessions over 2 weeks, then a booster every 4-6 weeks.
A 5-day ketamine-free break each month is recommended to prevent tolerance buildup.

sideEffects: Inability to function during and after dosing
Bladder inflammation risk with long-term use
